definition: The Treasury cash-futures basis trade at 50-100x leverage (~$800B-$1T current scale) absorbs cash Treasury duration, economically replicating QE at a scale comparable to a medium-sized purchase program. It provides pro-cyclical liquidity that unwinds during stress (March 2020, October 2023), suppressing term premium by an estimated 20-40bp. Scale has grown from $200-300B during QT1 to $800B-$1T currently.
evidence: Current basis trade size: ~$800B-$1T at 50-100x leverage
Pro-cyclical unwinding during March 2020 and October 2023
Identified in pair_0 debate as most important structural observation
Absorbed roughly half of QT's duration redistribution
March 2020 unwind is sole episode at scale (n=1)
Brunnermeier-Pedersen liquidity spiral theory provides mechanism
open_questions: What is the threshold at which basis trade unwinding triggers systemic stress?
How much term premium is being suppressed by basis trade duration absorption?
What would trigger an unwind at current scale?
What is the actual term premium suppression magnitude?
Can the basis trade unwind without systemic contagion given 3-4x larger notional vs 2020?
